blockingIssues.description: The duplicate hero below the atlas was removed, but the fix moved the full hero payload above the graph inside `#atlas`, regressing the required graph-first default workspace.

`VaultCapacityModel` still uses legacy linear share math instead of the shipped √-weighted bidding model with the $100 floor, and the UI still renders visible 'per-wallet' wording that VAL-TOKEN-017 forbids.

Several allocation rows still expose generic/search links while the address/query specifics live only in hidden `source.note` metadata, so the shipped UI does not provide direct visible Dune/basescan verification for the displayed on-chain figures.
The `Whale Exclusive Products` card is wired to enterprise data-licensing analysis, producing a user-visible taxonomy mismatch and leaving one headline use-case surface internally inconsistent.
`page.tsx` dynamically imports `ProofModule`, but it is a Server Component, so the below-fold proof section is not actually lazy-loaded in Next App Router and the new test only checks source syntax.

appliedUpdates.description: Added a factual architecture note that dynamically importing a Server Component in Next App Router does not lazy-load the server component itself, so real below-fold deferral must cross a client boundary.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When a worker changes the landing page, hero, or shell, require explicit transcript-visible reads of the mandated design-system/screenshot artifacts before they can report `followedProcedure=true`, or require them to mark a deviation if they skip those inputs.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `correction-hero-deduplication-and-logotype-fix` found a landing/shell change handoff claiming `followedProcedure=true` even though the transcript skeleton showed no pre-edit read of `.factory/library/design-system.md` or screenshot artifacts required by the skill.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Normalize the corrected vault-cap language across AGENTS/skills/validation artifacts so the milestone never reintroduces 'per-wallet' wording: update the stale validation-contract reference to a first-vault 'per-wallet deposit cap' and remove the contradictory AGENTS phrasing that still says '(not per-wallet)'.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `correction-vault-policy-total-cap-and-bidding-model` found shared-state drift: `validation-contract.md` still mentions a 'first-vault per-wallet deposit cap', while mission AGENTS/skill guidance describes the corrected total-cap model yet still preserves negated 'per-wallet' language that workers can copy into the UI.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify on-chain verification procedure so workers either (a) may rely on mission-provided Dune exports when supplied, or (b) must record concrete Dune CLI commands in the handoff before reporting `followedProcedure=true`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `correction-on-chain-verified-allocations` found the worker using `/Users/test/dune_query_results.md` without any transcript-visible Dune CLI command even though the skill currently says to use the Dune CLI for on-chain verification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Relax or clarify the tradebetter-parity workflow so successful evidence-refresh work can use `FetchUrl` plus `agent-browser` screenshots/computed-style checks without being treated as procedural drift, or else require skipped `pinchtab` usage to be reported explicitly as a deviation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The pass review for `correction-tradebetter-shell-parity-refresh` found a successful parity-refresh session that used `FetchUrl` and `agent-browser` only, while the current skill still mandates `FetchUrl`, `pinchtab`, and `agent-browser` together.

rejectedObservations.observation: `SourceCue.note` is not rendered by `EvidenceHook`, so workers can hide citation detail in note fields.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in `.factory/library/content-model.md`, and both failing reviews that raised it pointed to the same known library note rather than new shared-state information

rejectedObservations.observation: There is no documented crosswalk between use-case research sections and token-utility card IDs.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous; the review proves one wiring mismatch but does not establish a single canonical mapping for every research section/card pair
